title: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
description: Sir Keir Starmer is planning a law which will mean that the UK government can adopt EU single market rules, without them being voted on in Parliament.

title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that, if only so much.

title: China’s Electrostate Is Poised to Win From War in the Middle East
description: A cluster of Chinese firms is poised to win big from the energy crisis set off by the war in the Middle East, building on booming investment in artificial intelligence.

title: ‘The Strangest Sleepover Ever’: Dutch Skeptical as Their Royals Visit Trump
description: The Dutch king and queen are set to stay at the White House on Monday night. At a time of trans-Atlantic tension, the Netherlands isn’t sure how it feels about that.

title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
description: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.

title: What to Know About Pope Leo’s Voyage to Africa
description: The pontiff will visit four countries on the world’s poorest continent, demonstrating his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.

title: Aiming at China, Malaysia Puts New Restrictions on Electric Cars
description: The move was a tacit acknowledgment of China’s dominance in the global market and the power of its automakers to keep prices low.

title: Why the Vatican and the White House Are on the Outs - The Free Press
description: Why the Vatican and the White House Are on the Outs  The Free Press

title: Why the chemistry industry is prioritizing an America-first trade policy
description: Many don’t realize that building a stronger, more affordable America is directly tied to the growth of US chemical production. National defense, energy independence, modern health care, food production, and most other supply chains depend on chemistry.

title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with American pontiff
description: President Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV on Sunday night, saying he didn't think the U.S.-born global leader of the Catholic Church is "doing a very good job."

title: In Myanmar, Even Flowers Stoke Fear
description: Myanmar’s generals have brutalized the country for decades. But they have come to dread garlands as a sign of resistance.

title: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat - The Washington Post
description: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat  The Washington Post

title: Dianna Russini Lands Intriguing Job Offer Amid Mike Vrabel Scandal
description: NFL insider Dianna Russini may lose her job with The Athletic, but she's already been offered a new gig.

title: Colin Cowherd Urges Raiders to Draft Fernando Mendoza's Indiana Teammate
description: The Raiders are expected to take Indiana QB Fernando Mendoza at No. 1 overall. But another Hoosier might be in play at No. 36.

title: NYC mayor touts ‘socialist’ wins in first 100 days alongside Bernie Sanders
description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani highlights major policy wins in his first 100 days, crediting a democratic socialist agenda as Bernie Sanders praises the approach.

title: CBS bashed for botching Rory McIlroy’s Masters-winning shots
description: There is nothing like watching Rory McIlroy win a back-to-back Masters for golf fans -- that is if you actually see the winning shots.

title: Nets accomplished their two season goals — with lottery fate all that remains
description: The Nets ended the season with a 136-101 loss in Toronto.

title: Former NFL player Chris Payton-Jones, 30, dead in a Florida car crash
description: Payton-Jones signed with the Lions, Titans, Vikings and Raiders, playing in 29 games over six seasons in the league. He recorded 48 tackles and seven passes defended in the NFL.
